description: Strike a 'kuma' and its tensioned surface or resonant body answers with a drum sound. Playing the instrument is 'wile' (play), and 'telui' (rhythm) describes the pattern heard through its strokes. A 'teli' (bell) differs in the freely ringing tone of its hollow body.
articulatory_notes: Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for stressed unvoiced 'k' and release into high-back rounded 'u'. Bring the lips together for voiced 'm', keep the airflow through the nose, and open into central 'a'.
sound_symbolism: The first syllable closes at the back of the mouth around rounded 'u'; the second releases the lips into open 'a'. That compact-to-open movement can recall a stroke followed by the drum's resonance.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: A drum can keep shared work or dance in time, and its carrying sound can call people who are spread across a place. Neither use needs electrical amplification.

semantic_domains.aesthetic: a struck instrument whose pulse, tone, and resonance support rhythmic form
semantic_domains.creation: a made resonant instrument used to produce music
